source: ARE NEURAL RANKERS STILL OUTPERFORMED BY GRADIENT BOOSTED DECISION TREES

concepts.finding: LambdaMART (GBDT implementation LightGBM) substantially outperforms recent neural LTR models on benchmark datasets with numerical features. Neural models need: (1) log1p feature transformation, (2) data augmentation (Gaussian noise) for high-capacity networks, (3) self-attention for listwise context, (4) latent cross interactions. After these fixes, neural equals GBDT.
concepts.implication: For keyword-intent ranking with numerical features (frequency, tf-idf scores, etc.), LambdaMART/GBDT is a strong baseline that neural models may not beat without careful architecture. If the problem reduces to ranking keywords by intent relevance, tree-based methods remain state-of-the-art for tabular features.
concepts.open_question: For keyword clustering features (frequency, co-occurrence stats, n-gram overlaps), are tree-based or embedding-based models more effective?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: LambdaMART on engineered keyword features (co-occurrence statistics, entity type, length, etc.) will match or exceed neural embedding models for keyword-to-intent classification accuracy.
